assumptions: Please act as a professional Verilog designer.
A Read-Only Memory (ROM) module designed for storing fixed data. This module provides a way to access predefined data based on an 8-bit address input.
addr [7:0]: 8-bit address input used to select the data location in memory.
dout [15:0]: 16-bit output that delivers the data stored in the ROM at the specified address.
reg [15:0] mem [0:255]: Defines a ROM with 256 locations, each 16 bits wide.

The initial block is used to pre-load the ROM with fixed data. In this example, memory locations 0 through 3 are initialized with specific values (16'hA0A0, 16'hB1B1, 16'hC2C2, 16'hD3D3). Other locations can be initialized as needed.
The always @(*) block continuously outputs the data stored in the ROM at the memory location specified by addr.
Since ROM is read-only, this module includes only read operations.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Checks are limited to reset-safe, protocol-safe, or output-visible behavior.
No fixed latency or exact cycle count is assumed.
dout reflects the combinational value of mem[addr]
No dependence on clock or reset
cases.goal: Verify combinational read behavior of the ROM
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
The always @(*) block should produce dout immediately after addr changes
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known inputs: ['addr']
Drive a single legal addr value and observe dout
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Observe dout within the same delta cycle as the addr change (reset‑safe observation)

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
dout correctly outputs mem[0x00] and mem[0xFF]
Behavior is consistent for minimum and maximum address values
cases.goal: Exercise boundary addresses of the ROM
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Edge case includes the lowest (0x00) and highest (0xFF) address values
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['addr']
Use zero-like (0x00), one-like (0x01), and max (0xFF) patterns on addr
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Do not assume fixed latency; monitor dout after each address stimulus within a reset‑safe window

expected_properties: dout should reflect the memory content corresponding to each addr value presented in rapid succession
No glitches or undefined values should appear on dout during address transitions
cases.goal: Verify that consecutive address changes are handled correctly without assuming a fixed latency
notes: Since the ROM is combinational, dout is expected to follow addr changes within the same observation window
stimulus_intent: Apply a sequence of different addr values back-to-back without idle cycles
timing_assumptions: Do not assume a fixed number of cycles; monitor dout after each addr change within a reset‑safe window

unresolved_items: Timing model is unresolved; generated cases avoid fixed-latency checks.
Contract strength is limited; generated cases favor safe observation over precise timing assumptions.
Negative or illegal-input behavior is not planned because the contract does not provide reliable constraints.
